text:
Market today: The mood for your tech and chip names is sour, driven by Samsung's earnings falling short of high AI expectations. This has led to a sector-wide sell-off in chips.

Your holdings: SOXL is deep in the red at -35.06% and needs immediate attention given its leveraged nature and decay. SPAL, another leveraged new position, is also down significantly at -23.1%. QQQI is nearing its stop at 52. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on SOXL and SPAL for further downside, especially with chip weakness. Also, monitor QQQI for its stop trigger.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged and underperforming chip positions amidst sector-wide pressure.
plays: Consider trimming SOXL (-35.06%) and SPAL (-23.1%) to reduce exposure to leveraged, decaying positions amidst chip weakness.
Watch QQQI closely; trim if it breaks below its 52 stop level.
No new buys today – hold and watch for clearer market direction in tech.
